quick_skim.title: Adaptation of the hybrid fictitious domain-immersed boundary method for Reynolds-averaged turbulence modeling
quick_skim.abstract: Engineering practice often calls for shape or topology optimization (TO) of fluid defining components, while the ever-increasing computing power allows the optimized cost functions to be based on computational fluid dynamics (CFD). However, a common bottleneck in CFD-based TO frameworks is the requirement for frequent remeshing. In order to alleviate this bottleneck, we propose an adaptation of an immersed boundary (IB) method variant, the hybrid fictitious domain-immersed boundary method, to leverage Reynolds-averaged Navier-Stokes (RANS) equations and wall function. The main contribution of the present work lies in the design and open-source implementation of the IB-aware steady-state solution of the RANS equations via the SIMPLE algorithm in the OpenFOAM library. For the most common two-equation RANS models, Reynolds numbers from $10^1$ to $10^6$, and several benchmarks, such as flow over a backwards facing step or an Ahmed body, the framework gives results consistent with the standard body-fitted CFD. Furthermore, given the intended application in TO, special emphasis is placed on the robustness and applicability of the approach to general geometries, which is tested on a NACA profile under various angles of attack.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: adaptation of immersed boundary method for RANS-based shape/topology optimization of fluid components, including wall function for boundary layer modeling
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 浸入边界方法适配RANS方程用于流体部件的形状/拓扑优化，包括壁面函数以建模边界层
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 浸入边界方法适配RANS方程用于流体部件的形状/拓扑优化，包括壁面函数以建模边界层
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 浸入边界方法适配RANS方程用于流体部件的形状/拓扑优化，包括壁面函数以建模边界层
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an adaptation of the hybrid fictitious domain-immersed boundary method for RANS turbulence modeling to enable CFD-based shape optimization without frequent remeshing. Method incorporates wall functions for boundary layer treatment, facilitating aerodynamic shape optimization. Open-source implementation is provided.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对CFD基形状优化中频繁重划网格的瓶颈问题，本文将混合虚构域浸入边界方法适配至RANS湍流模型，并引入壁函数处理边界层。设计并实现了浸入边界感知的稳态RANS求解器，以OpenFOAM开源。该方法避免了复杂几何变形时的网格重构，可直接应用于气动外形优化，有效降低计算成本。

quick_skim.title: An Implicit Discrete Adjoint Gas-Kinetic Scheme for Aerodynamic Shape Optimization across all Mach Number Regimes
quick_skim.abstract: The gas-kinetic scheme (GKS) integrates the characteristics of flux difference scheme (FDS) and flux vector splitting (FVS) scheme, providing high accuracy in smooth regions and strong robustness near discontinuities across all Mach regimes. Leveraging these properties, an implicit discrete adjoint GKS is developed for aerodynamic shape optimization over a wide range of Mach numbers. The adjoint solver is constructed using the source-transformation-based algorithmic differentiation tool Tapenade. To enhance computational efficiency, both the flow and adjoint GKS equations are solved using an implicit time-marching strategy, also known as the Lower-Upper Symmetric Gauss-Seidel (LU-SGS) method. The effectiveness of the implicit formulation is demonstrated through comparisons with the explicit approach. To accurately impose solid wall boundary conditions, particularly in hypersonic regimes, kinetic boundary conditions and their adjoint counterparts are formulated for both adiabatic no-slip and isothermal walls. Four benchmark test cases covering subsonic, transonic, supersonic, and hypersonic flows are used to verify the effectiveness of the developed adjoint-based design optimization system.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: aerodynamic shape optimization using implicit discrete adjoint GKS
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 采用隐式离散伴随GKS进行气动外形优化

quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 采用隐式离散伴随GKS进行气动外形优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes an implicit discrete adjoint gas-kinetic scheme for aerodynamic shape optimization across all Mach regimes, achieving efficient gradient computation via algorithmic differentiation and implicit solving.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 针对全马赫数范围气动外形优化问题，提出一种隐式离散伴随气体动理学格式。利用源变换自动微分工具Tapenade构建伴随求解器，并结合LU-SGS隐式时间推进提升计算效率。数值实验验证了方法的有效性与鲁棒性，为跨速域飞行器气动设计提供了高效优化工具。

quick_skim.title: Bayesian optimization of stellarator alpha-particle confinement using data-informed parameter spaces and dimensionality reduction
quick_skim.abstract: Modern stellarators are typically designed by optimizing the shape of the plasma boundary surface, with the parameters taken to be Fourier amplitudes. Many promising optimization algorithms such as Bayesian methods require bound constraints on the parameters and are most efficient when each parameter is scaled similarly to the others. With the typical Fourier parameterization, it is unclear how to set these bounds: wide constraints lead to self-intersecting boundaries and frequent failures of the MHD equilibrium calculation, while tight bound constraints limit expressiveness. To address these issues, here we propose two new parameter spaces for stellarator optimization. Both begin with a dataset of existing stellarator boundaries. In the first approach, a quantile transformation is applied to each Fourier degree of freedom, mapping the data distribution to a uniform distribution on the unit interval. In the second approach, principal component analysis (PCA) is applied to points on the boundaries, followed by a quantile transformation. For both approaches, the transformed variables become the degrees of freedom, naturally bounded to [0, 1]. The PCA method has the additional benefit of dimensionality reduction, with high expressiveness for a small number of parameters. The methods are demonstrated via Bayesian optimization for good alpha-particle confinement with guiding-center tracing inside the optimization loop, using asynchronous parallelization. These optimizations yield stellarator configurations with excellent fast-particle confinement in fields that can be far from quasisymmetric or quasi-isodynamic.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: optimizing the shape of the plasma boundary surface
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 优化等离子体边界面的形状
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 优化等离子体边界面的形状
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 优化等离子体边界面的形状
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes new data-informed parameter spaces for stellarator shape optimization to set meaningful bound constraints, improving Bayesian optimization efficiency for alpha-particle confinement.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 仿星器优化设计通常以等离子体边界面的傅里叶振幅为参数，但难以合理设置参数边界，过宽导致自相交和平衡计算失败，过窄则限制形状表达。本文提出两种基于数据驱动的参数空间，从已有仿星器设计库学习跨微观维度并定义参数边界，同时结合主成分分析降维，使贝叶斯优化能够高效搜索约束良好的参数空间，成功优化α粒子约束，为聚变装置自动优化设计提供了实用方法。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 仿星器优化设计通常以等离子体边界面的傅里叶振幅为参数，但难以合理设置参数边界，过宽导致自相交和平衡计算失败，过窄则限制形状表达。本文提出两种基于数据驱动的参数空间，从已有仿星器设计库学习跨微观维度并定义参数边界，同时结合主成分分析降维，使贝叶斯优化能够高效搜索约束良好的参数空间，成功优化α粒子约束，为聚变装置自动优化设计提供了实用方法。

quick_skim.title: Optimal partition and segregation problems driven by torsional rigidity
quick_skim.abstract: Spectral optimal partition and segregation problems are deeply connected with harmonic maps, eigenfunctions, and the fine structure of nodal sets for linear elliptic equations. In this paper, we show that replacing the spectral energy by torsional rigidity leads to a genuinely different theory. The resulting optimal configurations are governed locally not by harmonic equations, but by torsion-type energies and unstable free boundary problems, thereby creating a natural bridge between optimal partition theory and the analysis of sublinear free boundary phenomena.   We prove existence of optimal torsional partitions and segregated torsional configurations, together with optimal Lipschitz regularity of the associated nonlinear eigenfunctions. We establish a strong unique continuation principle, characterize the admissible vanishing orders and the corresponding blow-up profiles, derive sharp Hausdorff dimension estimates for the nodal set and its singular subset, and prove $C^{1,α}$-regularity of the regular part of the free boundary. The proofs combine variational arguments with Almgren-type and Weiss-type monotonicity formulae adapted to the intrinsically sublinear torsional regime, blow-up analysis, and tools from geometric measure theory.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: optimal partition and free boundary problems driven by torsional rigidity
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 基于扭转刚度的最优划分与自由边界问题
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 基于扭转刚度的最优划分与自由边界问题
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 基于扭转刚度的最优划分与自由边界问题
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies optimal partition and segregation problems using torsional rigidity, linking them to torsion-type energies and sublinear free boundary phenomena, with existence and regularity results.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究以扭转刚度取代谱能量的最优划分与分离问题。这类最优构型受扭转型能量和不稳定自由边界问题支配，建立了最优划分理论与亚线性自由边界现象分析的桥梁。证明了最优扭转划分与分离构型的存在性，并得到相应非线性特征函数的最优Lipschitz正则性。成果丰富了自由边界问题的研究框架。

quick_skim.title: Anisotropic Parabolic Obstacle Problems and the Stefan Problem: Regularity of the Evolving Free Boundary
quick_skim.abstract: We study a parabolic obstacle problem for surfaces evolving by anisotropic mean curvature flow subject to an obstacle constraint. Given a convex obstacle and initial data, we seek an evolving surface minimizing an anisotropic energy functional while remaining above the obstacle; as a special case, this framework includes the anisotropic Stefan problem, where the free boundary represents a phase transition interface with direction-dependent surface tension. The central tool is the Cahn--Hoffman transform $S(x) = A^{-1/2}x$, which maps the Wulff ellipsoid $\{x : x^T A^{-1}x \leq 1\}$ to the Euclidean unit ball and converts the anisotropic problem into an equivalent isotropic one with a generalized Robin-type condition on the free boundary. We prove optimal regularity of the solution ($C^{1,α}$ in space and $C^{0,α/2}$ in time up to the free boundary) and $C^{1,α}$-regularity of the evolving free boundary at non-degenerate points. The parabolic Hausdorff dimension of the space-time singular set is shown to be at most $n - 1$.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: free boundary evolution via anisotropic mean curvature flow subject to an obstacle constraint, applicable to shape optimization by adjusting boundaries
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过各向异性平均曲率流受障碍约束的演化自由边界，适用于通过调整边界进行形状优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过各向异性平均曲率流受障碍约束的演化自由边界，适用于通过调整边界进行形状优化

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies anisotropic parabolic obstacle problems with free boundary evolution by anisotropic mean curvature flow and obstacle. Uses Cahn-Hoffman transform to analyze regularity, applicable to shape optimization via boundary adjustment.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 本文研究各向异性抛物型障碍问题以及各向异性Stefan问题中自由边界的正则性。问题设定为曲面在各向异性平均曲率流下演化，同时必须保持在给定的凸障碍之上，自由边界代表相变界面。核心工具是Cahn-Hoffman变换，它将各向异性Wulff椭球映射为单位球，从而将问题转化为等价的各向同性问题。通过分析转化后的Robin型边界条件，得到了自由边界的正则性结果，为通过边界演化进行形状优化奠定了理论基础。

quick_skim.title: Constraint-driven Optimization and Parametrization of Industrial NURBS Geometries via Neural Deformation Field
quick_skim.abstract: This work presents a differentiable framework for the parametrization and shape optimization of industrial CAD geometries represented by multi-patch NURBS surfaces. The method enables the deformation of complex CAD models through a physics-informed geometric parametrization, allowing direct morphing driven by physical constraints without the need to prescribe a predefined deformation strategy. A neural displacement field, implemented as a multi-layer perceptron acting on the NURBS control points, provides a compact parametrization of the admissible design space while preserving patch connectivity. Global geometric quantities relevant to hydrostatic design, including displaced volume, wetted surface area and buoyancy centroid, are formulated as differentiable integral operators evaluated on the parametric domain. These quantities are computed through Gauss-Legendre quadrature combined with analytical B-spline derivatives for surface metric evaluation, allowing gradient propagation to the deformation parameters while limiting the computational overhead of automatic differentiation. The proposed framework operates directly on CAD representations without intermediate mesh generation. Numerical experiments on a modified KVLCC2 hull demonstrate the capability of the method to satisfy competing hydrostatic constraints while producing smooth CAD-compatible geometries and showing stable convergence across multiple random initializations.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: shape optimization of NURBS geometries by adjusting boundaries

quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对多片NURBS曲面表示的工业CAD几何形状优化问题，提出了一种基于神经变形场的可微框架。该方法通过一个作用于控制点的多层感知机提供紧凑的参数化，无需预定义变形策略，直接在物理约束驱动下变形复杂CAD模型。实验表明该框架能有效处理静水压设计中的全局几何量（如排水体积、湿表面积和浮力），为工业形状优化提供了灵活工具。

quick_skim.title: A New Level Set Formulation for Improved Dirichlet Eigenvalue Minimizers
quick_skim.abstract: This paper makes several improvements to existing level set based approaches to computing shape optimizers for the Dirichlet eigenvalues subject to a volume constraint. The most notable changes in formulation include an overhaul of the classical level set construction and root-finding procedures as well the use of a regularized approximation to the standard objective function. Our resulting computational minimizers are either comparable to or improvements on the best known minimizers from the literature. We conclude with a survey of subproblems within the field that may benefit from numerical experiments; these include the existence of cusps on the boundary, the end-behavior of eigenfunction weights in the p-parameterized problem, and the nature of Weyl asymptotics as they relate to the Pólya conjecture.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: level set shape optimization for Dirichlet eigenvalues
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 利用水平集方法进行Dirichlet特征值形状优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 利用水平集方法进行Dirichlet特征值形状优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 利用水平集方法进行Dirichlet特征值形状优化
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes improvements to level set methods for computing optimal shapes that minimize Dirichlet eigenvalues under volume constraint, yielding better minimizers.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对带体积约束的Dirichlet特征值形状优化问题，提出了一种改进的水平集方法。通过重新设计经典的水平集构造和求根过程，并采用正则化目标函数近似，新方法计算出的形状极小化子与文献报道的最佳极小化子相比，要么相当，要么更优。论文还进一步探讨了该数值框架可能受益的若干子问题，包括边界尖端的存在性、p-参数化问题中特征函数权重的渐近行为，以及形状优化中某些定性问题，为未来实验提供了方向。

quick_skim.title: Geometry-Aware Anisotropic Boundary Correction for Aerodynamic Simulation
quick_skim.abstract: Aerodynamic simulation is a key component of engineering shape design, where core quantities such as the surface pressure coefficient strongly depend on flow dynamics near solid boundaries. Neural operators provide an efficient alternative to expensive Computational Fluid Dynamics (CFD) solvers. However, conventional methods treat the boundary region isotropically, failing to account for the distinct physical behaviors along the boundaries. In reality, the aerodynamic process exhibits anisotropy: along the tangential direction, flow propagates along the wall; along the normal direction, physical quantities are constrained by the wall. To explicitly model the distinct physical behaviors, we propose GeoABC, a geometry-conditioned anisotropic boundary correction framework. GeoABC leverages the boundary geometries to introduce direction-aware boundary correction into the intermediate representations of neural operators, transforming boundary geometry from static input features into a structural prior that modulates physical prediction. On 2D airfoil and 3D car tasks, GeoABC consistently adapts to multiple neural operator backbones, reducing near-boundary relative $L_2$ error by $\sim$38\% on average, narrowing the structural near-wall gap shared by mainstream neural operators, and advancing neural operators toward high-fidelity aerodynamic simulation.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: models anisotropic flow behavior near solid boundaries to correct aerodynamic simulation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 对空气动力学仿真中固体边界附近的各向异性流动行为进行建模修正
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 对空气动力学仿真中固体边界附近的各向异性流动行为进行建模修正
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 对空气动力学仿真中固体边界附近的各向异性流动行为进行建模修正
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes GeoABC, a geometry-conditioned anisotropic boundary correction for neural operator-based aerodynamic simulation, improving accuracy near walls by distinguishing tangential and normal flow behaviors.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对空气动力学仿真中传统方法在边界区域各向同性处理的不足，提出了一种几何条件化的各向异性边界修正方法GeoABC。该方法通过区分沿壁面的切向流动和受壁面约束的法向物理量，显式地建模不同的物理行为，从而提高了神经网络算子在近壁区的仿真精度。实验表明，该方法能更准确地捕获表面压力系数等关键量，对工程设计中的形状优化具有重要价值。

quick_skim.title: Distributional Loss for Robust Classification
quick_skim.abstract: This paper proposes a novel loss concept for supervised classification tasks. Rather than enforcing a direct mapping from each input sample to a single assigned label, we define an optimization objective over all classifier outputs as a bimodal Gaussian distribution. This softer target formulation implicitly captures class ambiguity, mitigates overfitting, and encourages the learning of more robust decision boundaries, all without requiring additional label information. Experimental results demonstrate consistent improvements in robustness, with particularly pronounced gains in low-data regimes, while requiring only minimal modifications to standard training pipelines.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: learning robust decision boundaries via distributional loss
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过分布损失学习鲁棒决策边界
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过分布损失学习鲁棒决策边界
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过分布损失学习鲁棒决策边界
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes a distributional loss for classification that models classifier outputs as a bimodal Gaussian, implicitly improving decision boundary robustness without extra label information.

quick_skim.title: A simulation study on spatial exponential decay of perturbations in a two-dimensional wave equation with optimal boundary/line control
quick_skim.abstract: Recent results have shown that domain-uniform stabilizability and detectability imply spatial exponential decay of perturbations in optimally controlled hyperbolic PDEs on one-dimensional domains. This domain-uniform stabilizability and detectability can be achieved only if the control domain is distributed over the whole spatial domain such that the distance between neighboring control intervals is bounded from above. Motivated by these insights we investigate whether analogous effects can be observed for optimal boundary control in higher dimensions. For this purpose we conduct a simulation study on a two-dimensional wave equation on expanding square domains which is driven by a localized perturbation of the initial displacement. We compare two control geometries: a control acting only on the outer boundary and a control acting on a regular grid of interior line interfaces combined with the boundary. The problem is discretized by conforming P1 finite elements in space and the implicit midpoint rule in time. Instead of assembling the full space-time KKT system, we use a condensed formulation and solve the reduced optimality system by preconditioned conjugate gradients. We show that domain-uniform spatial decay of the perturbation can only be observed in the scenario with a regular grid of line controls. This is because finite propagation velocity requires a uniform bound on the distance that a wave can travel without reaching the control domain.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: optimal boundary control for 2D wave equation
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 二维波动方程的最优边界控制
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 二维波动方程的最优边界控制
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 二维波动方程的最优边界控制
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies spatial exponential decay in optimally boundary-controlled 2D wave equation via simulation, exploring effects of localized control on expanding domains.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 基于一维域上的理论结果，研究二维波动方程最优边界控制下扰动的空间指数衰减特性。通过扩张方形区域的仿真实验，分析局部边界控制的影响。该工作将最优边界控制的稳定性分析拓展到高维，为分布参数系统的边界优化提供了仿真依据。

quick_skim.title: Optimization with inequality constraints by the embedded gradient vector field method
quick_skim.abstract: We develop a geometric framework for constrained optimization problems with inequality constraints through the introduction of quadratic slack variables. This formulation makes it possible to employ the language of Riemannian geometry and to solve the problem via the embedded gradient vector field method. We lift the feasible set to a smooth submanifold of an extended ambient space. The stratified structure of the resulting constraint manifold is analyzed in detail, yielding a natural partition according to which constraints are active. Using the embedded gradient vector field formalism, we derive explicit, determinantal formulas for the Lagrange multiplier functions directly from the geometry of the constraint manifold, recovering and re-framing the classical Karush-Kuhn-Tucker first-order necessary conditions without invoking the classical Lagrange multiplier method. Second-order optimality conditions are obtained by computing the restricted Hessian on each stratum, and a complete sign condition on the Lagrange multipliers is identified as the geometric counterpart of the classical complementary slackness condition. The theory is illustrated on the double ice-cream cone example, where the geometry of the problem determines the nature and number of local minima.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: geometric framework for inequality constrained optimization using embedded gradient vector field
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 使用嵌入梯度向量场的几何框架处理带不等式约束的优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 使用嵌入梯度向量场的几何框架处理带不等式约束的优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 使用嵌入梯度向量场的几何框架处理带不等式约束的优化

quick_skim.title: A Dual ADMM Framework for Inverse Parameter Identification in Elliptic Variational Inequalities
quick_skim.abstract: This paper is concerned with an inverse parameter identification problem governed by a second-kind elliptic variational inequality involving a non-differentiable boundary functional. The objective is to reconstruct an unknown Robin-type coefficient from boundary measurements while preserving the intrinsic nonsmooth structure of the underlying model. The inverse problem is formulated as a constrained optimization problem, where the cost functional measures the discrepancy between the computed and observed data and is stabilized by Tikhonov regularization. To overcome the difficulties associated with the nonsmooth variational inequality, we reformulate the state problem as a constrained minimization problem and employ an augmented Lagrangian framework combined with the alternating direction method of multipliers (ADMM). Fenchel duality is used to treat the non-differentiable boundary term without introducing smoothing approximations. We establish identifiability of the unknown coefficient and prove existence and convergence results for the proposed approximation scheme. Numerical experiments performed on a classical benchmark problem confirm the theoretical analysis and demonstrate that accurate parameter reconstruction can be achieved with reduced computational effort. The proposed methodology provides a robust framework for inverse problems arising in applications such as heat transfer, frictional contact, and corrosion detection.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: reconstructs unknown Robin-type boundary coefficient from boundary measurements via optimization
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 通过优化从边界测量重建未知Robin型边界系数
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 通过优化从边界测量重建未知Robin型边界系数
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 通过优化从边界测量重建未知Robin型边界系数
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes dual ADMM framework for inverse parameter identification in elliptic variational inequalities with nondifferentiable boundary functional, reconstructing Robin coefficient from boundary data.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文针对椭圆变分不等式中的逆参数识别问题，目标是从边界测量重建未知的Robin型系数。该问题涉及不可微边界泛函，因此被构造为约束优化问题并用对偶ADMM框架求解。通过引入Tikhonov正则化和增广拉格朗日法，有效地处理了非光滑性，并通过数值实验验证了方法的有效性。

quick_skim.title: Adaptive Hard-Soft Physics-Informed Neural Networks for Robust Boundary-Constrained PDE Solving
quick_skim.abstract: Physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) provide an effective way to solve partial differential equations (PDEs) by embedding physical principles into the learning process. However, the conventional PINN formulation, in which all constraints are imposed as soft penalty terms within a composite loss, often exhibits slow convergence, sensitivity to loss weight scaling, and inaccurate boundary enforcement due to poor conditioning of the optimization landscape. To address these limitations, this study proposes a unified hard--soft physics--informed neural network (HSPINN) with adaptive loss weighting. In this framework, Dirichlet and periodic boundary conditions are enforced exactly by construction through analytical or polynomial lifting, masking functions, and periodic feature mappings, while the governing PDE residuals, Neumann fluxes, and initial conditions are treated as soft constraints. An inverse-share softmax strategy dynamically balances the relative importance of individual loss components during training, eliminating manual penalty tuning and improving gradient stability. This formulation ensures boundary admissibility throughout optimization and enhances convergence efficiency and numerical robustness. Applications to representative elliptic (Poisson), parabolic (Burgers), and hyperbolic (convection with periodic boundaries) problems demonstrate that HSPINN consistently achieves faster convergence, higher accuracy, and greater stability than conventional PINNs, establishing a general and scalable foundation for physics-constrained deep learning across science and technology.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: exactly enforces Dirichlet and periodic boundary conditions to improve optimization of PDE solutions under boundary constraints
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 精确施加Dirichlet和周期边界条件以改善边界约束下PDE求解的优化
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 精确施加Dirichlet和周期边界条件以改善边界约束下PDE求解的优化
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 精确施加Dirichlet和周期边界条件以改善边界约束下PDE求解的优化

quick_skim.title: Geometry-Anchored Transport Framework for Exemplar-Free Class-Incremental Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Exemplar-free class-incremental learning (EFCIL) requires stable decision boundaries within a shifting feature space. While maintaining class-conditional Gaussian statistics provides a principled classification strategy, these parametric summaries remain sensitive to anisotropic representation drift. Existing methods often transport these statistics across tasks using a decoupled, post-hoc paradigm: optimizing a backbone without explicit geometric constraints can distort the legacy manifold, limiting the precision of retroactive alignment. In this paper, we formulate feature transport as an endogenous training constraint rather than a separate post-task step, presenting the Geometry-Anchored Transport Framework. First, we derive an Analytic Geometric Anchor via Mahalanobis-aligned regression to mitigate macroscopic anisotropic drift. Second, we introduce a Topology-Aware Evolution objective that regularizes localized manifold degradation while calibrating a residual network against the analytic prior. By coupling manifold evolution with transport constraints during the primary training phase, our framework mitigates evaluation errors without requiring decoupled fine-tuning. Experiments across CIFAR-100, TinyImageNet, and ImageNet-100 demonstrate that the proposed framework consistently improves upon existing post-hoc alternatives under strict exemplar-free constraints.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: optimizing decision boundaries in class-incremental learning
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 优化类增量学习中的决策边界

quick_skim.title: Anisotropic Obstacle Problems for Minimal Surfaces: Regularity of the Free Boundary via the Cahn-Hoffman Transform
quick_skim.abstract: We study an obstacle problem for surfaces minimizing an anisotropic surface energy of ellipsoidal type. Given a convex obstacle and a boundary datum, we seek a surface that minimizes the anisotropic area functional while remaining above the obstacle. The central novelty is the systematic use of the Cahn-Hoffman transform to convert the anisotropic problem into an equivalent isotropic problem with a generalized Robin boundary condition. We prove optimal regularity of the solution ($C^{1,1}$ up to the free boundary) and $C^{1,α}$-regularity of the free boundary itself under a non-degeneracy condition. The singular set of the free boundary is shown to have Hausdorff dimension at most $n-1$, and a logarithmic epiperimetric inequality yields its $(n-1)$-rectifiability. The approach combines Caffarelli's classical theory of obstacle problems with the geometric theory of anisotropic mean curvature and the Alexandrov reflection principle adapted to the anisotropic setting.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: minimizes anisotropic area functional under obstacle constraint, optimizing the free boundary shape
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 在障碍约束下最小化各向异性面积泛函，优化自由边界形状

quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Studies obstacle problem for anisotropic minimal surfaces, establishes optimal regularity of solution and free boundary via Cahn-Hoffman transform, enabling shape optimization.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 该论文研究了一类带有障碍约束的各向异性极小曲面问题，旨在最小化椭圆型各向异性面积泛函。通过系统运用Cahn-Hoffman变换，将原问题转化为具有广义Robin边界条件的各向同性问题，从而证明了自由边界的最优正则性。结果展示了自由边界的C^{1,α}正则性和奇性集维数的估计，为通过调整边界来优化形状提供了严格的理论基础。

quick_skim.title: Second-Order Area/Volume-Preserving PFEMs for Surface Diffusion via Simpson--Boole Geometric Identities
quick_skim.abstract: We propose second-order-in-time parametric finite element methods for surface diffusion of closed curves in two dimensions and closed surfaces in three dimensions. The construction is based on exact geometric variation identities along a quadratic temporal interpolation path. The induced area variation in 2D is evaluated exactly by Simpson's rule, while the induced volume variation in 3D is evaluated exactly by Boole's rule. The resulting fully discrete schemes preserve the enclosed area or volume exactly, without introducing an auxiliary Lagrange multiplier for the geometric constraint. They can be assembled on BGN-predicted auxiliary geometries and are therefore compatible with existing second-order BGN-type implementations. Numerical experiments demonstrate the expected second-order behavior, area/volume conservation, and good mesh quality for both curve and surface evolutions.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: surface diffusion governs boundary evolution that minimizes area under volume constraint, directly optimizing shape boundaries
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 表面扩散通过边界演化最小化面积，直接优化形状边界

quick_skim.title: Penalty-Free Natural Deep Ritz Method Based on de Rham Complex for High-Dimensional Dirichlet Boundary Value Problems
quick_skim.abstract: Deep neural networks show great promise for high-dimensional PDEs, yet enforcing essential boundary conditions remains challenging, especially as penalty parameters require problem-specific retuning with increasing dimensionality. In this work, we extend the Natural Deep Ritz Method (NatDRM) [H. Yu and S. Zhang, J. Comput. Phys., 537 (2025)] to a unified framework for all dimensions $d \geq 2$ based on the de Rham complex and its penalty-free boundary decomposition: curl-type operators act on scalar potentials in 2D, vector potentials in 3D, and antisymmetric second-order tensor potentials in $d \geq 4$, respectively. This method converts Dirichlet constraints into three coupled natural (Neumann-type) subproblems with corresponding Ritz-type losses, eliminating the need for a boundary penalty parameter $β$. We derive dimension-unified discrete losses, lightweight boundary-based gauge-fixing regularizations to resolve curl-kernel non-uniqueness, and a joint training procedure; extensions to variable-coefficient elliptic and semilinear Poisson problems are formulated at the first subproblem level. Numerical experiments on smooth benchmarks up to 6D show that NatDRM, without any penalty tuning, matches or exceeds the accuracy of optimally tuned DRM and PINN in most cases. It converges stably in 6D where penalized DRM fails for most penalty values, and exhibits synchronous decay of interior and boundary errors, resolving the inherent imbalance of penalty-based methods.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: enforces essential boundary conditions in high-dimensional Dirichlet boundary value problems penalty-free
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 无惩罚地强制执行高维Dirichlet边值问题的本质边界条件
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 无惩罚地强制执行高维Dirichlet边值问题的本质边界条件
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 无惩罚地强制执行高维Dirichlet边值问题的本质边界条件
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Extends Natural Deep Ritz Method to high-dimensional Dirichlet problems using de Rham complex, converting Dirichlet constraints into coupled Neumann-type subproblems without penalty parameters.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 深度神经网络求解高维偏微分方程时，如何高效满足本质边界条件是一大挑战，传统惩罚法需要针对不同维度和问题反复调节惩罚参数。本文基于de Rham复形将Natural Deep Ritz方法推广为适用于所有维度的统一框架，通过curl型算子对势函数的作用实现无惩罚边界分解，将Dirichlet约束转化为三个耦合的自然边界子问题，无需任何可调参数即可自动满足边界条件，在高维Dirichlet边值问题中表现出高精度和鲁棒性，为深度学习方法求解复杂边值问题提供了新途径。

quick_skim.title: Continuous-time Optimal Stopping through Deep Reinforcement Learning
quick_skim.abstract: Simulation based solvers for optimal stopping problems must discretize the stopping decision. Under classical dynamic programming, a coarse exercise grid with only a few stopping opportunities can materially undervalue the optimal expected reward, whereas on a very fine grid, approximation errors accumulate through the backward recursion. To remove this limitation, we develop a new reinforcement-learning inspired algorithm that enables us to learn the exercise rule at arbitrarily fine time resolution. Our CARLOS (Continuous-time Adaptive Reinforcement Learning for Optimal Stopping) algorithm utilizes an aggregate deep neural network (ADNN) to learn a joint space-time decision boundary. Starting from a coarse time grid, we progressively increase the frequency of stopping opportunities, while in parallel training the ADNN to refine its timing-value estimates. We moreover design an adaptive sampling strategy that gradually concentrates training effort near the stopping boundary. Benchmarked results show that CARLOS delivers higher prices than existing Bermudan solvers, approaching the American upper bound, and achieves high computational efficiency relative to non-RL comparators.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: learns a joint space-time decision boundary for optimal stopping
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 学习最优停止的联合时空决策边界
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 学习最优停止的联合时空决策边界
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 学习最优停止的联合时空决策边界
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Proposes CARLOS, a reinforcement learning algorithm that learns the exercise boundary in continuous time for optimal stopping, refining adaptively from coarse grids.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 最优停止问题是金融工程中确定最优执行时间的经典难题，传统基于动态规划的仿真求解器必须离散化停止决策，然而粗网格低估价值，细网格则因反向递推积累误差。为此，本文提出一种受强化学习启发的CARLOS算法，采用聚合深度神经网络（ADNN）学习联合时空决策边界，从粗时间网格开始逐步提高频率，实现在任意精细时间分辨率下学习执行规则，既避免了离散化精度损失，又摆脱了反向递推的误差累积，显著提升最优停止问题的求解精度和效率，对美式期权定价等场景具有重要实践价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 最优停止问题是金融工程中确定最优执行时间的经典难题，传统基于动态规划的仿真求解器必须离散化停止决策，然而粗网格低估价值，细网格则因反向递推积累误差。为此，本文提出一种受强化学习启发的CARLOS算法，采用聚合深度神经网络（ADNN）学习联合时空决策边界，从粗时间网格开始逐步提高频率，实现在任意精细时间分辨率下学习执行规则，既避免了离散化精度损失，又摆脱了反向递推的误差累积，显著提升最优停止问题的求解精度和效率，对美式期权定价等场景具有重要实践价值。
quick_skim.matched_query_text: how to optimize decision boundaries for classification

quick_skim.title: ConRTF: Edge-Constrained Boundary Distribution Refinement for Realtime TransFormer Table Structure Recognition
quick_skim.abstract: Table Structure Recognition (TSR) aims to recover the row and column layout of tables from document images, a key step in document understanding pipelines. Accurate TSR depends on precise boundary localization: small errors in row or column boundaries can propagate into incorrect cell assignments and structural inconsistencies. Yet detection-based approaches treat table elements as generic objects, ignoring a fundamental property of table layout: rows and columns play structurally distinct roles and their boundaries carry unequal importance. We propose an Edge-constrained Fine-grained Localization loss (EFL) that formalizes this structural asymmetry by encoding table-specific geometric priors into the training objective: row-like elements are supervised with emphasis on their horizontal boundaries, while column-like elements prioritize vertical boundaries. Implemented within a real-time detector with distribution-based boundary refinement (D-FINE), EFL operates during training only and guides boundary refinement toward structurally meaningful adjustments with no change to the inference pipeline. The proposed approach, ConRTF, is also data-efficient, maintaining robust accuracy with as few as 2k--3k annotated tables. Experiments on PubTables-1M and two private datasets show consistent improvements over the optimized baseline and several real-time detectors including RT-DETRv2 and YOLOv10-11, with gains of up to +1.6 GriTS points at equal inference speed.
quick_skim.llm_evidence_en: edge-constrained boundary distribution refinement for table structure recognition
quick_skim.llm_evidence_cn: 边缘约束的边界分布精炼用于表格结构识别
quick_skim.llm_evidence: 边缘约束的边界分布精炼用于表格结构识别
quick_skim.canonical_evidence: 边缘约束的边界分布精炼用于表格结构识别
quick_skim.llm_tldr_en: Addresses precise boundary localization in table structure recognition by proposing an edge-constrained fine-grained localization loss that encodes table-specific geometric priors, improving boundary accuracy.
quick_skim.llm_tldr_cn: 表格结构识别依赖精确的边界定位，微小误差会导致单元格分配错误。本文提出边缘约束的精细定位损失函数，利用行和列的结构不对称性，编码表格特定的几何先验，优化边界分布。实验表明，该方法显著提升了边界精度和整体识别性能，展示了边界优化在文档分析中的价值。
quick_skim.llm_tldr: 表格结构识别依赖精确的边界定位，微小误差会导致单元格分配错误。本文提出边缘约束的精细定位损失函数，利用行和列的结构不对称性，编码表格特定的几何先验，优化边界分布。实验表明，该方法显著提升了边界精度和整体识别性能，展示了边界优化在文档分析中的价值。
